things.name: Belem Tower & Jeronimos Monastery
things.description: Twin UNESCO World Heritage Manueline masterpieces on the Tagus — monuments to the Age of Discovery

things.name: Alfama & Sao Jorge Castle
things.description: Lisbon's oldest neighbourhood — Moorish castle, labyrinthine lanes and the sound of fado at night

things.name: Tram 28 Ride
things.description: Iconic yellow tram rattling through the narrow streets of Alfama, Graca and the historic districts

things.name: MAAT Museum of Art, Architecture & Technology
things.description: Striking riverside contemporary art museum in a wave-shaped building at Belem

things.name: Sintra Day Trip
things.description: UNESCO World Heritage town with fairy-tale palaces in the mountains — Pena, Quinta da Regaleira

things.name: Time Out Market
things.description: Lisbon's most exciting food hall in the 1892 Mercado da Ribeira — 35 restaurants under one roof

things.name: National Tile Museum (Azulejo)
things.description: Unique museum in a 16th-century convent dedicated to Portugal's beloved painted ceramic tile tradition

things.name: Bairro Alto & Chiado
things.description: Bohemian hilltop quarter with independent bookshops, fado houses, street art and sunset bars

things.name: Gulbenkian Museum
things.description: One of Europe's finest private art collections — Egyptian, Islamic and Impressionist masterpieces

things.name: Pasteis de Belem
things.description: The original 1837 pastry shop making the world-famous pastel de nata custard tarts using a secret recipe
